description:
Goal: the work graph should expose the behaviors the design depends on: exact waits, coalescing, blocking, fact changes, and bounded convergence. Signal: FactPattern is gone, waits are exact-key O(1), AppliedStep reports changed/enqueued/coalesced/blocked work from one seam, and non-converging fact values fail with a precise diagnostic instead of hanging the drive loop. Strategy: delete speculative wait-pattern generality, return complete applied-step data from Scheduler::complete, emit cheap raw telemetry from World::complete_job, and add a per-fact fingerprint-change guard grounded in lattice height.

Change:
- Replace FactPattern/ExactPattern with exact FactKey wait maps.
- Extend AppliedStep with coalesced and blocked work.
- Emit raw job/fact/coalesced/blocked events from World::complete_job using AppliedStep; handlers may format if they care.
- Add per-drive fact churn accounting and SEMANTIC_NONCONVERGENCE for facts that exceed their family height bound.

Why:
- Waits are exact today; the trait and linear scan are unused generality on a hot path.
- The engine already knows what changed and what work moved; Compiler2 should observe that uniformly instead of sprinkling special events.
- A fixpoint compiler needs a legible floor for the one residual case static recursion facts cannot prove.

Checks:
- Scheduler tests cover exact waits, coalesced follow-ups, blocked waits, and changed facts.
- Compiler2 trace-summary uses AppliedStep events as its daily signal.
- A higher-order recursive non-convergence repro emits a fatal diagnostic naming the unstable fact.
title: Compiler2 engine: exact waits, applied-step signal, and convergence guard
